title: Centratherum reticulatum

Within this tribe, Centratherum reticulatum is further classified under the genus Centratherum. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Its classification is used for identification and understanding its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

intro: Centratherum reticulatum, also known as the Lasiandra or Reticulated Centratherum, is a flowering plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Centratherum reticulatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
